adversarial_check.description: Mandatory self-referential audit beyond the 9 listed commands, per the coordinator's three-part instruction (this requirement is itself now codified in 02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 8, the doc under test). (a) Compared 04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention against this evaluator's own 014-qa-01..09.txt files: found a genuine partial non-compliance -- their first line is a '(script: qNN.sh)' label rather than the exact invocation, and the invocation truly executed embedded a forbidden session-absolute /tmp path (the command BODIES remain repo-relative and replayable, but line 1 does not satisfy Sec 5's letter); cross-checked against features 000-011's evidence files, which DO comply exactly, showing this is a real regression introduced around feature 012-013. Logged honestly as a corrective item; does not affect this contract's deliverable correctness. (b) Cross-checked 00_planning.md Sec 9's Plan Amendment Rule against .agent/run_state.json's actual history entries for features 008/009/010 and the 012 re-baseline: all four match the rule's orchestrator-registrable-amendment pattern (human-authorized, cites a recorded history event, no Planner routing needed) and the 012 re-baseline specifically satisfies the structural scope_budget.rebaseline requirement verbatim. (c) Applied 02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 7's External Anchor Rule retroactively to qa/002.json's adversarial_check: confirmed it is correctly classified as a violation -- its expected-value fragments were read out of standard/AGF.md itself (the artifact under test) rather than an external source, exactly the failure mode Sec 7 targets, and exactly why that check could not detect AGF.md's later-corrected (feature 010) verdict-shape drift.
